name: Seasoned Green Olives- Country Living
recipeIngredient: 1 Lemon, halved, seeded and cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1 tbsp Dried oregano or 3 T chopped fresh oregano
recipeInstructions:
1. With the flat side of a meat mallet or rolling pin, crush each olive just to crack the flesh. Place the cracked olives in a quart jar. Add enough cold water to cover the olives and insert a small, sealed plastic food-storage bag filled with 1/4 cup water to keep the olives immersed. Seal the jar; refrigerate for 3 weeks, changing the water in the jar every day.
2. After 3 weeks, sample an olive – it should be only slightly bitter. Continue to soak the olives in water if they are still very bitter.
3. When the olives are ready, heat 2 cups of water to boiling in a quart saucepan. Add salt and stir to dissolve. Set the brine aside to cool to room temperature.
4. Drain the olives and rinse them in cold water. Place the cured olives in a clean quart jar. Cover the olives with cooled brine. Stir in vinegar, lemon cubes, oregano, and garlic. Top with olive oil and seal the jar. Refrigerate the olives for at least 1 month to develop flavor. The olives will keep for 2 more months refrigerated. Drain before serving.
